question:
Will the price of Ethereum be above $3,300 on November 17?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Market Quick Take - 13 November 2025 | Saxo
evidences.content: The next catalysts for crypto are likely to come from US CPI, Treasury issuance, and renewed visibility on economic activity now that government data releases resume. [...] Digital assets are mixed this morning, with bitcoin steady around USD 103.7k and ether recovering above USD 3.5k, helped by a mild improvement in risk appetite following the end of the US government shutdown. Alt-coins show a selective drift higher, with XRP and Solana in positive territory, while broader sentiment remains cautious and sensitive to incoming US inflation data. ETF flows remain a focal point. IBIT slipped to USD 57.59, reflecting a day of light outflows across bitcoin ETFs, while ETHA trades around USD 25.79, consolidating after recent volatility. Despite uneven flows, institutional interest has not disappeared; rather, it appears more measured as investors digest the macro backdrop. [...] Equities: US healthcare and AI chips lifted the Dow to new records, Europe broke to fresh highs, Asia mostly firmer with Japan and Hong Kong up Volatility: calm conditions, CPI in focus, SPX expected move ±53, downside skew persists Digital Assets: BTC steady, selective alt-coin strength, IBIT/ETHA consolidat...

evidences.title: SEC Approves Eight Ethereum ETFs
evidences.content: The SEC has given the green light to eight spot Ethereum ETFs through an omnibus order, a decision that follows just four months after the agency approved spot Bitcoin ETFs for the first time. The ETFs, from BlackRock, Fidelity, Grayscale, Bitwise, VanEck, Ark, Invesco Galaxy, and Franklin Templeton, received approval for their 19b-4 forms.
evidences.query: Ethereum ETF BlackRock Fidelity Grayscale SEC ETH spot ETF flows staking unlocks Binance ETHUSDT

evidences.title: BlackRock, Fidelity and six other firms receive SEC approval for ...
evidences.content: UPDATED 18:54 EDT / MAY 23 2024 ### BlackRock, Fidelity and six other firms receive SEC approval for Ethereum ETFs by Duncan Riley In a surprise decision, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission announced today that it has approved eight applications for spot Ethereum exchange-traded funds. The SEC approved 19b-4 forms — submissions made to the SEC seeking approval to list and trade a new ETF — that detailed the proposed rule changes and regulatory compliance. The eight submissions approved cover ETFs from BlackRock Inc., Fidelity, Grayscale Investments Inc., Bitwise Asset Management Inc., VanEck Associates Corp., Ark Investment Management LLC, Invesco Capital Management LLC and Franklin Templeton. [...] Ethereum ETFs are a type of investment fund that tracks the price of Ethereum. They’re traded on traditional stock exchanges rather than cryptocurrency exchanges, allowing investors to invest in Ethereum without holding actual cryptocurrency. An Ethereum ETF represents shares in a fund that has Ethereum as its primary asset, with investment values rising and falling in tandem with the changes in the price of Ethereum. The decision by the SEC comes after it gave approval to s...

evidences.title: SEC Approves Spot Ethereum ETFs - RankiaPro
evidences.content: This week, several firms amended their applications to include prohibitions on staking. Updated filings from CBOE, the exchange for ETFs from Fidelity, VanEck, Invesco, Ark21Shares, and Franklin Templeton, now state: 'Neither the trust, nor the sponsor, nor the custodian, nor any other person associated with the trust will, directly or indirectly, engage in action where any portion of the trust’s ETH becomes subject to the ethereum proof-of-stake validation or is used to earn additional ETH or generate income or other earnings.' Other firms have included similar language to ensure compliance with the SEC's requirements. ## Future of Spot Ethereum ETFs [...] logo separator separator # SEC Approves Spot Ethereum ETFs Imagen del autor 24 MAY, 2024 By Jose Luis Palmer from RankiaPro Europe featured LinkedIn LinkedIn Twitter Twitter Mail Mail The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved rule changes that will allow exchanges to begin listing spot ethereum ETFs from several major firms, including BlackRock, Ark Invest, and Fidelity. The decision, announced late Thursday, marks a significant development in the cryptocurrency investment landscape. ## Pending Final Approvals...

evidences.title: Standard Chartered analysts raise Ethereum year-end ...
evidences.content: The firm’s analysts now forecast Ethereum at $7,500 by year-end, up from $4,000, and $25,000 by the end of 2028, up from the previous $7,500 target. “We now see ETH at USD 7,500 by end-2025 (USD 4,000 prior) and USD 25,000 by end-2028 (USD 7,500),” the firm's analysts wrote. Geoffrey Kendrick, the firm's Head of Digital Assets Research, expects ETH to surpass its prior all-time high of $4,866 by the end of Q3. ## The catalysts In a report shared with The Block, Kendrick says ETH treasury companies and spot ETFs have purchased roughly 3.8% of all ether in circulation since early June. That’s almost double” the fastest comparable pace seen in Bitcoin from similar buyers. [...] Standard Chartered has been vocal on its dedicated ETH treasury thesis, arguing in prior commentary that public-company balance sheets, institutional spot products, and policy clarity can make Ether “very investable” for traditional allocators. It even argued that digital asset treasury firms could be more attractive than U.S. spot ETH ETFs. The latest upgraded outlook formalizes that view into explicit price targets and quantifies a demand shock driven by treasuries and ETFs that, if sustained, could keep s...

evidences.title: Ethereum (ETH) Price Prediction 2026 2027 2028 - 2040 - Changelly
evidences.content: ## FAQ ### What Is the Prediction for Ethereum in 2026? Analyst projections range widely: Benzinga’s aggregated data places ETH between $3,000 and $6,000 in bullish conditions. InvestingHaven suggests a $2,500–$5,500 band. Citi has cited $4,500, while Standard Chartered has referenced $7,500 under stronger institutional participation. Machine-learning models estimate an average near $4,359. ### How Much Will 1 Ethereum Be Worth in 2030? Analyst projections range widely. Finder’s expert panel projects a long-term price of $10,000+ for ETH by 2030, as adoption and staking participation expand. Other 2030 projections span a huge range: [...] In 2031, Cryptopolitan forecasts a minimum ETH price of $21,942, an average around $22,545, and a potential high of $25,611, reflecting Ethereum’s increasing appeal to investors and its role as global financial and digital infrastructure. Even more conservative analysts acknowledge that ETH’s trajectory over five years is fundamentally tied to whether it maintains dominance in smart contracts and DeFi. Ethereum is considered a strong long-term investment due to its dominance in smart contracts, staking rewards, and continuous innovation. If tha...

evidences.title: Weekly Crypto Market Wrap: 13th October 2025 - Zerocap
evidences.content: Altcoin activity picked up meaningfully, with XRP, ADA and AVAX drawing notable buying interest on the back of strong narratives and ecosystem momentum. In contrast, ETH, HYPE, SEI, OM, FLUI and L3 saw concentrated selling, particularly in SEI and HYPE where risk sentiment has turned. This split between accumulation in majors and trending alts versus broader de-risking elsewhere underscores a market that is selectively deploying capital into weakness. Overall, flows reflect selective risk deployment and evolving liquidity dynamics, with capital consolidating into majors and key narratives while peripheral names face sustained selling pressure. [...] Bitcoin’s euphoric breakout above $125k early last week ended violently on Friday as a new front opened in the U.S.-China trade war. Trump’s announcement of a 100% tariff on Chinese tech exports, alongside export controls on software, sparked a record $19B liquidation cascade across crypto markets (this single-day liquidation was ~10x greater than during COVID and FTX’s insolvency event). BTC collapsed from ~$117k to $110k within minutes, ETH fell 16% below $3,600, and high-beta alts were hit hardest, many shedding 20–50%. Within an...

evidences.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options expiry seen as a potential volatility catalyst
evidences.content: Analysts said volatility had cooled after the prior week's turmoil, while warning that calm may not last. The options market showed mixed sentiment, with earlier demand for short-dated puts as traders hedged risk and continued interest in longer-dated Ethereum calls extending into 2026, suggesting longer-term optimism amid near-term uncertainty and macro event risk.

evidences.title: Background on Ethereum's long-term value thesis
evidences.content: Ethereum is described as a major settlement layer for tokenized assets, stablecoins, and DeFi, creating structural demand for the asset. The snippet also notes that part of Ethereum transaction fees are burned, which can limit supply growth and potentially make the asset deflationary as network usage increases. It mentions competition from other blockchain networks and argues that some rivals compete more directly with Ethereum layer-2 networks than with Ethereum itself.
